world.title: French ship crosses Strait of Hormuz in first Western European transit during Iran war
world.description: A vessel owned by France’s CMA CGM has become the first ship tied to Western Europe to cross the Strait of Hormuz since the outbreak of the Iran war in late February, according to ship tracking data.

world.description: CEPA warns Russia moves from covert influence to sabotage, attacks, and hybrid aggression across NATO and EU territory, raising global security risks.

world.description: Three other teenagers have been charged over Chloe's death. She was found in the street suffering from stab wounds.

business.title: Kospi rebounds nearly 3 percent as hope grows for Strait of Hormuz agreement
business.description: Korean stocks soared by nearly 3 percent on Friday, as Iran's discussions with Oman on a protocol to monitor traffic through the Strait of Hormuz boosted hopes of easing oil supply disruptions despite heightened tensions in the Middle East.

business.title: How One FIRE Couple Saved More Than Half Their Income to Retire Early

business.title: 1 Value Stock to Keep an Eye On and 2 We Avoid
business.description: The low valuation multiples for value stocks provide a margin of safety that growth stocks rarely offer. However, the challenge lies in determining whether these cheap assets are genuinely undervalued or simply on sale due to their potentially deteriorating business models.

business.description: IT Sector Q4 Results Preview: Tier-2 IT companies are expected to continue to outperform their Tier-1 counterparts. Kotak Institutional Equities expects TCS to lead revenue growth among Tier-1 companies, while Persistent Systems will lead the charge among mid-tier companies.

business.title: MTN separates mobile money into standalone fintech in Ghana

science.description: Four Artemis astronauts were zooming towards the Moon late Thursday after a major engine firing, a milestone that commits NASA to the first crewed lunar flyby in more than half a century.

science.title: Maternal parent sets the pace: Imprinted genes linked to faster drying in hybrid maize seeds
science.description: A research team found that hybrid maize (Zea mays) is often harvested with high seed moisture, increasing drying costs and risking mechanical damage and reduced vigor.

science.title: Large Traces of Common Earth Element Found on Mars. Is This a Proof of Ancient Life on the Red Planet?
science.description: While the researchers aren't saying they found actual Martians or proof of life, they are saying the planet was stocked with the right nutrients. It’s like finding a fully stocked pantry in an abandoned house; we don't know if anyone ever lived there, but we do know there was plenty for them to eat.

science.title: This tiny claw in a 500-million-year-old fossil just rewrote the origin of spiders
science.description: What started as routine fossil cleaning turned into a major scientific surprise when researchers uncovered a tiny claw in a 500-million-year-old specimen where no claw should exist. That detail revealed Megachelicerax cousteaui, the oldest known relative of spiders, pushing the origins of this group back by 20 million years. The fossil shows that key features of modern spiders and horseshoe crabs were already emerging during the Cambrian Explosion.

science.title: NASA's Artemis II and four astronauts heading to the moon : NPR
science.description: With the last major firing of its engine, the Artemis II spacecraft is now on a path that will take it around the moon and back.
